title: Public and Partner Operations
text: Public and Partner Operations Operations can be modified using the public or partner modifier. Public operations can be invoked from other components. Partner operations can only be invoked from the component in which they are defined. Partner operations are typically used for making functional decompositions, similar to functions. They do not appear in the signature repository. Partner operations look similar to functions, but have a number of differences: Partner operations have a name scope; in other words, unlike functions, you can define a partner operation with the same name in different containers, such as in an Operations container and an Occurrence Operations container. Partner operations can be activated universally using a handle and the -> operator. Functions must be called using the call ProcScript statement. Example: Public Operation with XML Stream public operation getAccounts params xmlStream [dtd:account.olb] x: inout endparams xmlLoad x, "dtd:account.olb" retrieve/e "accountSsv.olb" xmlSave x, "dtd:account.olb" end; operation getAccounts Copy Related Links Handles A handle is a reference to an object, such as a synchronous component instance, entity, occurrence, or field. Handles can be used to call operations on the referenced object, or to pass these objects as variables and parameters. Script Container Location for defining the ProcScript modules of the associated object. Collection Script Container Code container of an entity that is used for operations that handle the collection of entity occurrences (as opposed to individual occurrences). Occurrence Script Container Code container for defining occurrence-level operations and triggers. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
